name: garf-executors

metadata.name: garf-executors
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Executes queries against API and writes data to local/remote storage.

documentation: # Gaarf Executors

modules.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.cli.bq
documentation:
Module for defing `garf-bq` CLI utility.

`garf-bq` allows to execute BigQuery queries based on Garf config.

functions.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.extract_datasets
documentation:
Finds dataset-related keys based on values in a dict.

If dict contains keys with 'dataset' in them, then values for such keys
are treated as dataset names.

Args:
    macros: Mapping containing data for query execution.

Returns:
    Possible names of datasets.

classes.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutorError
documentation: Error when executor fails to run query.

classes.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutor

methods.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutor.__init__
documentation:
Initializes BigQueryExecutor.

Args:
    project_id: Google Cloud project id.
    location: BigQuery dataset location.

methods.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutor.client
documentation: Instantiates bigquery client.

methods.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutor.execute
documentation:
Executes query in BigQuery.

Args:
    script_name: Script identifier.
    query_text: Query to be executed.
    params: Optional parameters to be replaced in query text.

Returns:
    DataFrame if query returns some data otherwise empty DataFrame.

methods.name: garf_executors.bq_executor.BigQueryExecutor.create_datasets
documentation:
Creates datasets in BQ based on values in a dict.

If dict contains keys with 'dataset' in them, then values for such keys
are treated as dataset names.

Args:
    macros: Mapping containing data for query execution.

Handles query execution in BigQuery.

Attributes:
    project_id: Google Cloud project id.
    location: BigQuery dataset location.
    client: BigQuery client.

modules.name: garf_executors
documentation:
Defines simplified import of executors.

Instead of importing `garf_executors.api_executor.ApiQueryExecutor`
import like this `garf_executors.ApiQueryExecutor`

modules.name: garf_executors.api_executor
documentation:
Module for executing Garf queries and writing them to local/remote.

ApiQueryExecutor performs fetching data from API in a form of
GarfReport and saving it to local/remote storage.
Copyright 2024 Google LLC
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

classes.name: garf_executors.api_executor.ApiQueryExecutor

methods.name: garf_executors.api_executor.ApiQueryExecutor.__init__
documentation:
Initializes QueryExecutor.

Args:
    fetcher: Instantiated report fetcher.

methods.name: garf_executors.api_executor.ApiQueryExecutor.aexecute
documentation:
Reads query, extract results and stores them in a specified location.

Args:
    query_text: Text for the query.
    query_name: Identifier of a query.
    customer_ids: All accounts for which query will be executed.
    writer_client: Client responsible for writing data to local/remote
        location.
    args: Arguments that need to be passed to the query.
    optimize_performance: strategy for speeding up query execution
        ("NONE", "PROTOBUF", "BATCH", "BATCH_PROTOBUF").

methods.name: garf_executors.api_executor.ApiQueryExecutor.execute
documentation:
Reads query, extract results and stores them in a specified location.

Args:
    query_text: Text for the query.
    query_name: Identifier of a query.
    writer_client: Client responsible for writing data to local/remote
        location.
    args: Arguments that need to be passed to the query.

Gets data from API and writes them to local/remote storage.

Attributes:
    api_client: a client used for connecting to API.

classes.name: garf_executors.sql_executor.SqlAlchemyQueryExecutor

methods.name: garf_executors.sql_executor.SqlAlchemyQueryExecutor.__init__
documentation:
Initializes executor with a given engine.

Args:
    engine: Initialized Engine object to operated on a given database.

methods.name: garf_executors.sql_executor.SqlAlchemyQueryExecutor.execute
documentation:
Executes query in a given database via SqlAlchemy.

Args:
    script_name: Script identifier.
    query_text: Query to be executed.
    params: Optional parameters to be replaced in query text.

Returns:
    DataFrame if query returns some data otherwise empty DataFrame.

Handles query execution via SqlAlchemy.

Attributes:
    engine: Initialized Engine object to operated on a given database.

functions.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.convert_date
documentation:
Converts specific dates parameters to actual dates.

Returns:
    Date string in YYYY-MM-DD format.

Raises:
    ValueError:
        If dynamic lookback value (:YYYYMMDD-N) is incorrect.

functions.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.initialize_runtime_parameters
documentation:
Formats parameters and add common parameter in config.

Initialization identifies whether there are `date` parameters and performs
necessary date conversions.
Set of parameters that need to be generally available are injected into
every parameter section of the config.

Args:
    config: Instantiated config.

Returns:
    Config with formatted parameters.

functions.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils._remove_empty_values
documentation: Remove all empty elements: strings, dictionaries from a dictionary.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfQueryParameters
documentation:
Annotation for dictionary of query specific parameters passed via CLI.

Attributes:
    macros: Mapping for elements that will be replaced in the queries.
    template: Mapping for elements that will rendered via Jinja templates.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.BaseConfig

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.BaseConfig.__add__
documentation:
Creates new config of the same type from two configs.

Parameters from added config overwrite already present parameters.

Args:
    other: Config that could be merged with the original one.

Returns:
    New config with values from both configs.

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.BaseConfig.from_dict
documentation: Builds config from provided parameters ignoring empty ones.
Base config to inherit other configs from.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfConfig

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfConfig.__post_init__
documentation: Ensures that values passed during __init__ correctly formatted.

Stores values to run garf from command line.

Attributes:
    account:
        Account(s) to get data from.
    output:
        Specifies where to store fetched data (console, csv, BQ.)
    api_version:
        Google Ads API version.
    params:
        Any parameters passed to Garf query for substitution.
    writer_params:
        Any parameters that can be passed to writer for data saving.
    customer_ids_query:
        Query text to limit accounts fetched from Ads API.
    customer_ids_query_file:
        Path to query to limit accounts fetched from Ads API.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfConfigException
documentation: Exception for invalid GarfConfig.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfBqConfig
documentation:
Stores values to run garf-bq from command line.

Attributes:
    project:
       Google Cloud project name.
    dataset_location:
        Location of BigQuery dataset.
    params:
        Any parameters passed to BigQuery query for substitution.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfSqlConfig
documentation:
Stores values to run garf-sql from command line.

Attributes:
    connection_string:
       Connection string to SqlAlchemy database engine.
    params:
        Any parameters passed to SQL query for substitution.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.ConfigBuilder

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.ConfigBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Sets concrete config type.

Args:
    config_type: Type of config that should be built.

Raises:
    GarfConfigException: When incorrect config_type is supplied.

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.ConfigBuilder.build
documentation:
Builds config from file, build from arguments or both ways.

When there are both config_file and CLI arguments the latter have more
priority.

Args:
    parameters: Parsed CLI arguments.
    cli_named_args: Unparsed CLI args in a form `--key.subkey=value`.

Returns:
    Concrete config with injected values.

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.ConfigBuilder._build_config
documentation:
Builds config from named and unnamed CLI parameters.

Args:
    parameters: Parsed CLI arguments.
    cli_named_args: Unparsed CLI args in a form `--key.subkey=value`.
    init_defaults: Whether to provided default config values if
        expected parameter is missing

Returns:
    Concrete config with injected values.

methods.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.ConfigBuilder._load_config
documentation:
Loads config from provided path.

Args:
    garf_config_path: Path to local or remote storage.

Returns:
    Concreate config with values taken from config file.

Raises:
    GarfConfigException:
        If config file missing `garf` section.

Builds config of provided type.

Config can be created from file, build from arguments or both.

Attributes:
    config: Concrete config class that needs to be built.

classes.name: garf_executors.entrypoints.utils.GarfParamsException
documentation: Defines exception for incorrect parameters.
